verses.text: God summons the nations to be silent and answer: who raised up the conqueror from the east? 'I the LORD, the first... and the last.' He alone directs history. The God who calls and ends all things is sovereign over every empire.

God summons the nations to be silent and answer: who raised up the conqueror from the east? 'I the LORD, the first... and the last.' He alone directs history. The God who calls and ends all things is sovereign over every empire.

God summons the nations to be silent and answer: who raised up the conqueror from the east? 'I the LORD, the first... and the last.' He alone directs history. The God who calls and ends all things is sovereign over every empire.
The frightened isles encourage one another and hurry to nail down their idols lest they topple. Fear drives them to false gods. Men in dread cling to the very idols that cannot save them.

But to Israel His servant God says, 'Fear thou not; for I am with thee... I will strengthen thee; yea, I will help thee.' This is the believer's comfort. The presence and upholding hand of God banish every fear.

But to Israel His servant God says, 'Fear thou not; for I am with thee... I will strengthen thee; yea, I will help thee.' This is the believer's comfort. The presence and upholding hand of God banish every fear.

But to Israel His servant God says, 'Fear thou not; for I am with thee... I will strengthen thee; yea, I will help thee.' This is the believer's comfort. The presence and upholding hand of God banish every fear.
All who rage against His people shall be ashamed and perish, 'for I the LORD thy God will hold thy right hand.' God grasps His own. Held by His hand, the weakest need not fear the strongest foe.

'Fear not, thou worm Jacob'; God will make the trembling people 'a new sharp threshing instrument' to thresh the mountains. He turns weakness to strength. The God who calls us worms can make us mighty in His work.

When the poor and needy seek water and find none, 'I the LORD will hear them,' opening rivers in the desert. He supplies the thirsty. God meets the needs of His people with abundance from unlikely places.

God challenges the idols to declare things to come or do good or ill; they are 'nothing,' and those who choose them an abomination. The gods are exposed. What cannot foretell or act is no god at all.

He raised up one from the north to tread on rulers, while the idols are but 'wind and confusion.' God alone declared it beforehand. Only the LORD foretells and fulfils; the idols are emptiness.
